dry_bulk.confluence_note: A sharp disconnect exists between extreme quantitative strength (BDI at 99.7th percentile) and persistently negative fundamental assessments from analysts.
dry_bulk.summary: Spot rates for Capesize and Panamax vessels have surged despite China's iron ore portside inventories hitting all-time highs and steel production remaining in contraction. The market is currently driven by price momentum rather than structural demand, as evidenced by declining coal imports and subdued industrial indicators.
dry_bulk.outlook: Neutral-to-Bearish as high inventories and macro slowdown risks threaten to cap the current spot rally.
dry_bulk.watch: China steel production data and iron ore inventory drawdown rates at major ports.

tanker.confluence_note: Extremely high Z-scores in clean tankers and positive fundamental sentiment align, though recent momentum has cooled from 'explosive' to 'negative' as the market digests recent gains.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is undergoing a structural shift as unnavigable straits and Middle East conflict force alternative routing, dramatically increasing ton-mile demand. Clean tankers are trading at 100% of their 5-year percentile, supported by drawing inventories and elevated crude prices. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2202.0 (ROC60 +156.9%), alongside dirty tankers at 2865.0 (ROC60 +76.2%).
tanker.outlook: Strongly Bullish driven by geopolitical disruptions and extended transit times.
tanker.watch: Navigability status of major straits and Middle East conflict escalation.

macro_note: The shipping complex is bifurcated: tankers are thriving on geopolitical-driven supply chain inefficiencies, while dry bulk is facing a fundamental ceiling due to Chinese industrial fatigue and record commodity stockpiles.
